title: Akums to acquire Oriflame India's manufacturing business
summary: Akums Drugs and Pharmaceuticals will acquire Oriflame India's manufacturing business. This significant deal is valued at fifty-six crore rupees for the company. The acquisition includes two manufacturing facilities and a leased warehouse. Akums aims to...
thbAction: Use the article as an entry point to pitch THB's commercial visibility layer for territory planning, account prioritization, and next-best-action for pharma leadership teams.
contentHtml:
New Delhi: Akums Drugs and Pharmaceuticals on Thursday said it will acquire the manufacturing business of Oriflame India Ltd for Rs 56 crore, marking its entry into colour cosmetics.

The company's wholly-owned subsidiary, Pure and Cure Healthcare Pvt Ltd, has received board approval to acquire the manufacturing business of Oriflame India, Akums Drugs and Pharmaceuticals Ltd said in a statement.

The transaction covers two manufacturing facilities -- one at Roorkee, Uttarakhand, and the other at Noida, Uttar Pradesh, along with a leased warehouse situated at Noida -- for Rs 56 crore, it added.

The acquisition is being made purely on a cash consideration basis and is expected to be completed by August 31, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions, the company said.

"This acquisition is a significant step in Akums' strategy to diversify beyond pharmaceuticals into high-growth adjacent categories," Akums Drugs and Pharmaceuticals MD Sanjeev Jain said.

Cosmetics, colour cosmetics in particular, represent one of the fastest-growing consumption stories in India today, he noted.

"Acquiring these two well-established manufacturing facilities gives us immediate, high-quality capacity and capability, and strengthens our ability to serve both Indian and global brands," Jain said.

The facilities being acquired bring a wide range of manufacturing capabilities across skincare, wellness, hair care and colour cosmetics, the company said.
